tests_not_run: 原始报告为只读审查输出；未运行项保留在 finding.required_tests。

findings.title: changed-scope still fail-opens invalid explicit base into an empty successful scope

evidence.result: Static path: scripts/validate_project_governance.py:1438-1440 only diffs an explicit base when git_ref_exists(base) is true; unresolved explicit bases fall through without an error.

Executed CLI: python -B scripts\lean_governance.py changed-scope --base-ref refs/heads/DOES_NOT_EXIST_M1_R2_A5 returned exit 0, stderr 0 bytes, stdout 286 bytes, changed_files=[], selected_project_count=0.

Executed control: python -B scripts\lean_governance.py ci --changed-only --base-ref refs/heads/DOES_NOT_EXIST_M1_R2_A5 returned exit 1 only because sync validation emitted Explicit governance diff base does not resolve to a commit; the embedded changed_scope object still reported empty scope.

findings.impact: Any candidate check-plan or shadow classifier that consumes changed-scope directly can convert an unresolved base into a successful zero-project plan, violating INV-006 and CE-001 even though the higher-level ci wrapper currently exits nonzero.
findings.recommended_fix: Make unresolved explicit base a first-class error from the shared diff helper. changed-scope should return nonzero or emit unresolved_base with a non-success decision; ci should not publish selected_project_count=0 for an unresolved base.
required_tests: changed-scope invalid explicit base returns nonzero or structured unresolved_base with non-success decision
ci invalid base remains nonzero and its changed_scope/check_plan section cannot be mistaken for a valid empty diff
candidate check plan consumes the same fail-closed base resolution result as legacy validation

findings.title: Root governance changes still exclude two required projects from changed-scope shadow coverage

evidence.result: governance/projects.yaml declares changed_scope_excluded_projects: EEI and arxiv-daily-push; both projects have ci_mode: required.

Executed unittest set included ProjectGovernanceValidatorTests.test_other8_s2pct01_current_root_test_change_excludes_parallel_projects; the test asserts selected_project_count=8 and excludes EEI and arxiv-daily-push for tests/governance/test_project_governance_validator.py root change.
The 5-method direct unittest run returned exit 0 in 0.113s, confirming the current expected behavior is to exclude those two required projects.

reproduction: Inspect governance/projects.yaml root_governance.changed_scope_excluded_projects and projects ci_mode fields
findings.impact: A root governance implementation or test change can exercise only 8 of 10 required projects in the ordinary changed-only path. Shadow acceleration can make that under-coverage look intentional through budget telemetry instead of fixing the required coverage gap.
findings.recommended_fix: Remove required projects from root changed-scope exclusions, or add machine-proven equivalent required companion checks before PROCEED_SHADOW. The candidate should treat excluded required projects as a blocking condition, not as savings.
required_tests: Root governance file changed fixture covers every ci_mode=required project or fails with explicit companion-proof absence
selected_project_count excludes required projects only when a required companion job/artifact is machine-proven
Budget telemetry labels required-project exclusions separately from safe scan avoidance

findings.title: check-render unknown project still leaks a Python traceback instead of compact actionable output

evidence.result: Executed CLI: python -B scripts\lean_governance.py check-render --project DOES_NOT_EXIST_M1_R2_A5 returned exit 1, stdout 0 bytes, stderr 1096 bytes.
stderr began with Traceback and ended at scripts/lean_governance.py:241 ValueError: Unknown project: DOES_NOT_EXIST_M1_R2_A5.
CE-008 expected a nonzero actionable error with no Python traceback.

findings.impact: The candidate compact-output contract is not preserved on this common operator error. It increases token/noise cost and weakens the promised human-readable fast path.
findings.recommended_fix: Catch ValueError/SystemExit at lean_governance CLI boundaries for render/check-render and emit compact JSON or one-line errors that include the unknown selector and valid next actions, without traceback.
required_tests: check-render unknown project exits nonzero with no Traceback in stdout/stderr
render unknown project exits nonzero with no Traceback in stdout/stderr
Error output stays below the compact-output budget while preserving actionable selector information

findings.title: Current shadow CI output does not prove token/stdout reduction and is larger than legacy on the 0-project path

evidence.result: candidate_m1s objective is to move repeated governance computation/full JSON/telemetry out of ordinary stdout and reduce token and rerun cost.
Executed legacy comparison: python -B scripts\validate_project_governance.py --changed-only --enforce-sync --semantic returned exit 0 with stdout_bytes=209.
Executed candidate path: python -B scripts\lean_governance.py ci --changed-only returned exit 0 with stdout_bytes=1302 on the same clean 0-project state.

Executed 20 repeats of python -B scripts\lean_governance.py ci --changed-only: all status 0, stderr 0, stdout_bytes alternated 1301/1302, durations ranged 858-1067 ms; no persistent compute reuse was evidenced.

budget_telemetry reports proxy counts such as full_project_scan_avoided_count=10 but does not include legacy_stdout_bytes, candidate_stdout_bytes, baseline duration, cache key, or reused computation count.

reproduction: Repeat the lean_governance ci command 20 times and record status, stdout_bytes, stderr_bytes, duration_ms
findings.impact: The candidate can preserve legacy pass/fail yet still add ordinary-path token load and repeated computation. Without a measured ROI gate, PROCEED_SHADOW would approve an acceleration claim based on proxy telemetry rather than demonstrated savings.
findings.recommended_fix: Move full JSON evidence to runner temp or .git/codex-review and emit a smaller stdout summary with an evidence pointer. Add an ROI gate that compares candidate vs legacy stdout bytes and wall time for 0/1/8 project fixtures and representative real diffs.
required_tests: 0-project clean changed-only: candidate exit equals legacy exit and candidate stdout is below a declared legacy-relative or absolute budget
1-project changed-only fixture: candidate exit equals legacy exit and stdout/token budget is measured against legacy
8-project root governance fixture: required-project exclusions are not counted as savings unless companion proof exists
20-repeat stress records deterministic exits plus measured compute reuse or explicitly reports no reuse

findings.title: Diff-selection git subprocesses still lack per-command timeout and structured timeout errors

evidence.result: Static path: scripts/validate_project_governance.py:1408-1416 git_ref_exists uses subprocess.run without timeout.
Static path: scripts/validate_project_governance.py:1427-1463 git_changed_files uses subprocess.check_output for git diff/ls-files without timeout.

Executed external cancel probe: python -B scripts\lean_governance.py ci --changed-only with injected 10 ms parent timeout ended with status=null, signal=SIGTERM, error=spawnSync python ETIMEDOUT, stdout/stderr 0, post git status clean.
This external timeout proves the process can be killed cleanly in this run, but it does not provide the structured bounded git error required by CE-007.

reproduction: Inspect scripts/validate_project_governance.py git_ref_exists and git_changed_files subprocess calls
Run a parent-level timeout against python -B scripts\lean_governance.py ci --changed-only and observe that the application emits no structured timeout result
findings.impact: A hung git/diff operation can stall the governance path or be killed by the runner without preserving actionable fail-closed evidence. Candidate check-plan logic would inherit the same unbounded diff-selection behavior.
findings.recommended_fix: Introduce a shared bounded git helper with per-command timeout, UTF-8 decoding, stderr capture, and structured error propagation. Treat timeout as fail-closed for changed-scope, ci, and candidate check plan.
required_tests: Monkeypatch subprocess timeout for git_ref_exists and git_changed_files; command exits nonzero with structured timeout error
Timeout cannot produce changed_files=[] with a success decision
Cancellation leaves tracked status clean and writes only to allowed temp/evidence paths

findings.title: Workflow-level concurrency can still cancel full evidence runs on the same ref

evidence.result: .github/workflows/project-governance.yml sets concurrency.group to project-governance-${{ github.ref }} and cancel-in-progress: true.
The same workflow contains full governance scheduled/manual-all steps and ordinary changed-only pull_request/push steps under that single concurrency group.

Executed local two-instance read-only ci --changed-only probe: both instances returned code 0 with stdout_bytes=1302 and post tracked status clean, but this local probe does not exercise GitHub Actions cancellation semantics.

reproduction: Inspect .github/workflows/project-governance.yml concurrency and job if conditions
On GitHub Actions, start workflow_dispatch scope=all and then a same-ref fast run; verify whether the full evidence run is canceled
findings.impact: Scheduled/manual full evidence can be preempted by a same-ref fast run, undermining the candidate claim that full/nightly/release evidence gates remain intact while ordinary runs are accelerated.
findings.recommended_fix: Split concurrency groups by event/scope or disable cancel-in-progress for scheduled/manual full evidence. Keep ordinary fast-gate cancellation separate from full evidence production.
required_tests: Workflow config test proves full evidence scopes do not share a canceling concurrency group with fast changed-only runs
Live or mocked workflow_dispatch all followed by same-ref fast run does not cancel the full evidence run

findings.title: Zero tracked diff does not prove temp/cache cleanliness; ignored governance pycache already exists

evidence.result: Initial and post-run git status --short returned empty output.
git status --ignored --short tests\governance returned !! tests/governance/__pycache__/.

.github/workflows/project-governance.yml full test step runs python3 -m pytest tests/governance -q or unittest discover without PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE, PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX, or pytest cacheprovider suppression in that step.
The executed Agent5 tests used python -B and did not clean the existing ignored cache because RUN_MODE=READ_ONLY allowed only the final JSON write.

reproduction: Inspect .github/workflows/project-governance.yml full governance validator test step
findings.impact: Candidate zero-write telemetry that only checks tracked status can miss checkout-local ignored cache pollution. This weakens the READ_ONLY/CI zero-write invariant and makes temp cleanup status ambiguous.
findings.recommended_fix: In CI, set PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1, PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX=$RUNNER_TEMP, and disable pytest cache or redirect it to RUNNER_TEMP. Add an ignored-cache audit or explicit allowed-cache report for governance tests.
required_tests: Clean checkout full governance tests leave no __pycache__ or .pytest_cache under the checkout, or report allowed temp locations
pre/post status includes ignored-cache audit for tests/governance and root governance scripts
temp cleanup is separate from tracked zero-diff and reports NOT_RUN when deletion is forbidden
